rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.2 EQUITIES grid — Japan row = N (NC), prose 'taking a neutral view on the US and Japan'
rows.note: emitted-but-evidence-gated: model emitted Japanese equities->Japan Equities N (failures.csv reason=evidence_check_failed, source=monthly, p.2 EQUITIES grid). Grounded ONLY in monthly; quarterly dashboard has 'Asia developed markets - Neutral', not a Japan row (kept in output as Asia Equities N, an adjacent leaf). The print-captured monthly dial grid was rejected by the visual-token evidence gate, not misread.

rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.3 GOVERNMENT BONDS grid — US row = N (upward arrow, moved from UW to neutral)
rows.note: Model kept the same call under the adjacent leaf US Duration N (output.csv, locator p.3 GOVERNMENT BONDS grid). Same N view, same underlying US-rates call; exact-leaf join missed only because taxonomy leaf differs (US Treasuries vs US Duration). Not evidence-gated (this dial call survived and was kept).

rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.3 GOVERNMENT BONDS grid — Europe ex-UK row = N (downward arrow, moved from OW to neutral)
rows.note: Model kept the same call under the adjacent leaf Europe Duration N (output.csv, locator p.3 GOVERNMENT BONDS grid). Same N view; join missed only on leaf naming (Euro Govt Bonds vs Europe Duration). Not evidence-gated.

rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.3 GOVERNMENT BONDS grid — UK row = N (NC), prose 'Our neutral view on UK rates is unchanged'
rows.note: emitted-but-evidence-gated: model emitted UK rates->UK Duration N (failures.csv reason=evidence_check_failed, source=monthly, p.3 GOVERNMENT BONDS grid). Unlike US/Europe, the UK rates call was NOT recovered anywhere else in output (no UK rate call kept), so this is a genuine absent row driven by the evidence gate rejecting the print-captured dial grid.

rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.4 CREDIT summary box — 'A significant tightening in spreads has prompted us to shift our overall stance on credit to neutral ... across both investment grade and high yield'
rows.note: Grounded. Review flag stems from cross-doc arbitration (quarterly dashboard had credit Moderately OW; monthly, more recent as of 31 May 2026, moved to neutral) — model correctly took the more-recent monthly N. GT simply did not make a broad-credit call.

rows.cite: quarterly:printed.pdf p.7 — 'we favor European credit including investment-grade and high-yield bonds'
rows.note: Grounded, explicit. GT has no Europe Credit leaf.

rows.cite: quarterly:printed.pdf p.2 Key points — 'moderately overweight view on commodities, expressed in gold'; corroborated monthly p.4 COMMODITIES dial = OW(+)
rows.note: Grounded in both docs. GT did not score a Commodities aggregate leaf.

rows.cite: quarterly:printed.pdf p.3 Global equities dashboard — 'Asia developed markets — Neutral'
rows.note: Adjacent to GT's Japan Equities N (eval near-leaf-hint sim=0.333). Independently grounded in the quarterly dashboard row (Asia developed markets, which spans Japan/Aus/HK/Sing). Legitimate call that partially overlaps the Japan Equities GT miss.

rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.4 CREDIT grid — Investment-grade credit row = N (downward arrow)
rows.note: Grounded. Review flag = cross-doc conflict (quarterly credit-spreads dashboard = Global IG Moderately OW; monthly grid = N). Model applied recency and took monthly N — defensible. GT did not score Global IG.

rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.4 CREDIT grid — High yield row = N (downward arrow)
rows.note: Grounded. Same recency arbitration as IG (quarterly had Global HY Moderately OW). GT did not score Global HY.

rows.cite: quarterly:printed.pdf p.4 — 'Japan is the one market where we maintain a moderate duration underweight'
rows.note: Grounded in quarterly prose. CROSS-DOC CONFLICT the model did NOT arbitrate: monthly GOVERNMENT BONDS grid shows Japan = N (more recent). Model kept quarterly's U here while applying recency elsewhere (credit, US/Europe rates) — a minor grouping inconsistency. Not GT-scored so no eval impact.

rows.cite: quarterly:printed.pdf p.7 — 'a continued positive secular trend linked to our expectations for a weaker US dollar and a bias among central banks and investors to diversify away from the dollar'
rows.note: Grounded directional inference; review flag reflects inferred basis (Medium band), not lack of grounding. A weaker-USD expectation cleanly supports a USD underweight. GT has no USD leaf.

rows.cite: quarterly:printed.pdf p.6 — 'US mega-cap tech companies have seen a big adjustment in relative valuations, making them attractive again from a PE perspective'
rows.note: Grounded, directional. GT did not score a mega-cap leaf.

rows.cite: quarterly:printed.pdf p.7 — 'We have maintained our long-duration stance based on more attractive carry and two potential states of the world that could lead to lower yields'
rows.note: Grounded, explicit. GT did not score a generic long-duration leaf.

rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.3 GOVERNMENT BONDS grid — US row = N (upward arrow)
rows.note: This IS the near-leaf that covers the GT US Treasuries N miss (same call, adjacent leaf). Grounded in monthly dial + prose 'moved from an underweight to a neutral stance on US rates'.

rows.cite: monthly:printed.pdf p.3 GOVERNMENT BONDS grid — Europe ex-UK row = N (downward arrow)
rows.note: Near-leaf covering the GT Euro Govt Bonds N miss. Grounded in monthly dial + prose 'neutralised our stance for now'.

summary.grouping_note: Combining the two docs worked well overall. Cross-doc dedup handled numerous overlaps (US equities, EM, Europe, gold, global govt bonds all suppressed as duplicate_same_view in failures.csv). The one substantive conflict was credit: the quarterly credit-spreads dashboard printed Global IG/HY as Moderately OW, while the more-recent monthly (as of 31 May 2026) moved IG and broad credit to Neutral after spread tightening; the model correctly applied recency and kept the monthly N. One residual inconsistency: for Japan rates the model kept the quarterly's moderate duration UW (Japan Duration U) rather than the more-recent monthly grid's Japan = N, i.e. recency arbitration was applied to credit and US/Europe rates but not Japan. Not GT-scored, so no eval impact.
summary.headline: Wellington had zero view disagreements: every non-exact row is either grounded breadth, a taxonomy near-leaf, or an evidence-gate artifact — no reading errors. Of the 4 misses, 2 (US Treasuries N, Euro Govt Bonds N) were actually captured under adjacent duration leaves (near_leaf_covered), and the other 2 (Japan Equities N, UK Gilts N) were emitted by the model but rejected by the visual-token evidence gate on the print-captured monthly dial grid (emitted-but-evidence-gated) rather than missed on reading. All 12 model_only rows are grounded (9 sound_breadth, 3 near_leaf_of_gt), 0 overreach. The dominant fixable failure mode is the evidence gate rejecting the monthly HTML dial dashboard (7 monthly dial calls hit evidence_check_failed); recovering that grid would convert both recall_gap misses and add several corroborations.
